title: Ganjuko

'''Ganjukos''' were large creatures from [[Fenn (planet)|Fenn]] with beaks and thick coats of hair. They were known to stampede at a moment's notice. 

Their beaks were prized, as they could be crafted into expensive daggers, a sign of status among [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[clan]] leaders.<ref name="guide" />

==Biology and appearance==
Ganjuko hatched from [[egg/Legends|eggs]]. In the first 7 [[standard month/Legends|months]] of their lives the hatchings grew protected in a pouch on their mother's back, then they were abandoned to survive by themselves. The young had a high mortality rate and often fell prey to other predators; those who survived into adulthood become fierce predators close to the top of the food chain.<ref name="guide" />

The creatures had massive layers of fat covered in dark, thick fur in order to retain heat in frigid climates. Their hair varied from black to dark-brown, and their exposed parts varied from red to bright orange.<ref name="guide" />

Their skull plates and beaks were made of very hard cartilage and, other than protection, they were used for attacking. During fights, their eyes could be protected by retractable cartilage lids, preferring to use sound and smell over sight. They opened the armor plates of their prey, or dug, with their beaks.<ref name="CG" /><ref name="guide" />

They were diel, active during both days and nights, taking small naps all around the rotation. They were capable of jumping and climbing. In fact, they were uncomfortable after few hours in above-freezing temperatures, becoming gradually weaker and slower. After more than week in "warm" environments, they fell into hibernation until temperature was restored.<ref name="guide" />

During her lifetime, a female could [[oviparous|lay]] only 4 to 8 eggs in her lifetime. They had a lifespan of 110 years.<ref name="guide" />
==Habitat==
In [[pre-Republic era]], they were spread to other worlds in [[Dufilvian sector/Legends|Dufilvian sector]] as well as worlds in [[Bothan Space/Legends|Bothan sector]] such as [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]] and [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]].

The Ganjuko was a predator that thrived in the arctic regions of [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]]. They were solitary and territorial; carving or digging a nest in the ice or snow, they controlled and fiercely protected an area of at least 200 square km.<ref name="guide" />

{{Creature-stub}}
